name: Prevention's Perfect Kabobs
recipeInstructions:
SOAK 12 bamboo skewers in water.
1. Prepare marinade by blending all ingredients for 1 minute; use half as marinade and reserve the rest for later basting.
2. Cut chicken breast into 6 equal 1-1/2-inch pieces. Rinse scallops. Combine chicken and scallops with half cup yogurt marinade in a zipper-lock bag. Massage to coat; refrigerate for 1 hour.
3. In a saucepan, bring 4 quarts water to boil over medium-high heat. Cut potatoes in half. Peel carrots and cut into three 1-1/2-inch pieces from the thick ends. Trim leaves from brussels sprouts; wash sprouts.
Place potato halves in boiling water. After 1 minute, add carrot pieces; after another minute, add sprouts. Cook vegetables for 3 minutes. Add prunes and apricots to boiling water and cook for 1 minute; drain on a plate.
4. Wash and trim yellow squash into six 3/4-inch slices. Cut red bell pepper into six 1-1/2-inch chunks.
5. Using two 6-inch bamboo skewers per kabob, insert vegetables in parallel manner, one at a time, piercing (in order): carrot (sideways across the grain), prune, chicken, squash, red pepper, sprout (sideways across the grain), scallop, apricot, and potato (round end outside).
Make 5 more kabobs using the same method.
6. Preheat broiler or grill to high. Spray a broiler pan with non-stick spray and place about 3-4 inches from heat.
Place kabobs directly on hot grates and baste with reserved marinade. Broil for 7-9 minutes (grill for 5-6 minutes).
SERVE with Chunky Garden Yogurt Sauce and a salad: Prevention's Parsley Laden Tabbouleh; Spinach-Yogurt Salad
PANTRY:
FATTOUSH - Bread salad, based on a selection of greens and herbs (purslane, parsley, mint, arugula, sumac) blended with toasted pita bits and fresh tomatoes.
TABBOULEH - Salad of bulgur mixed with chopped tomatoes, onion, mint, parsley, olive oil, and lemon juice.
